<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
 So the root cause is that SQLAlchemy automatically expires all attributes on committed objects, and when the route tries to access those attributes later, it triggers lazy-loading that can't happen in a synchronous context. Setting `expire_on_commit=False` on the async_sessionmaker prevents this expiration and is the standard solution for async SQLAlchemy.</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

The error is happening because after `await db.commit()`, SQLAlchemy expires all attributes on the ORM objects by default. When your route then accesses `doc.id`, `doc.filename`, etc., it triggers a lazy-load via implicit IO, which can't run synchronously in an async context — hence the `MissingGreenlet` error.

The fix is to set `expire_on_commit=False` on your async `sessionmaker`. This is the recommended pattern for async SQLAlchemy and is mentioned explicitly in the docs.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

That should resolve the `MissingGreenlet` error on the upload endpoint. A few notes on what's going on and why this is the right fix:

### Why the error happened

```24:52:backend/app/routers/documents.py
@router.post("/upload", response_model=DocumentResponse, status_code=201)
async def upload_document(
    ...
):
    ...
    try:
        doc = await process_document(db, file.filename, pdf_bytes)
        await db.commit()
    except ValueError as e:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail=str(e))

    return DocumentResponse(
        id=doc.id,  # <-- expired attribute, triggers lazy load = sync IO in async context
        ...
    )
```

By default, `Session.commit()` calls `expire_all()`, marking every attribute on every managed instance as stale. The next attribute access (`doc.id`) tries to refresh by issuing a SELECT through the sync DBAPI shim of asyncpg, which requires the greenlet bridge that only exists inside `await`-driven calls — so SQLAlchemy raises `MissingGreenlet`.

### The fix

Setting `expire_on_commit=False` on the `async_sessionmaker` is the standard solution for `AsyncSession`. After commit, your instance attributes remain populated from the prior INSERT/SELECT, so building the response model doesn't trigger any implicit IO.

```19:27:backend/app/core/database.py
class DatabaseSessionManager:
    def __init__(self, host: str, engine_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {}):
        self._engine = create_async_engine(host, **engine_kwargs)
        self._sessionmaker = async_sessionmaker(
            autocommit=False,
            expire_on_commit=False,
            bind=self._engine,
        )
```

### A separate issue visible in your logs

Above the SQLAlchemy traceback, the actual root cause of the failed upload was:

```
openai.RateLimitError: Error code: 429 - 'You exceeded your current quota...'
```

So OpenAI rejected the embedding/classification request, your `process_document` raised, and somewhere along the unwind `doc.id` was accessed on the now-expired object. With `expire_on_commit=False` you won't see the misleading `MissingGreenlet`, but uploads will still fail with a 500 until you either:
- add credits / a working key to the OpenAI account, or
- switch the provider via `LLM_PROVIDER=ollama` (or anthropic) given you already have a `factory.py` with multiple providers.

Want me to also make `process_document` catch the OpenAI `RateLimitError` and surface it as a clean `HTTPException` (e.g. 503) instead of a 500?
